completeness_check.result: PASS — All 126 in-scope FDEs evaluated. Zero blanks.

rationale_consistency_check.rationale_type_justification: Semantic used as default per use case (vocabulary divergence between general-purpose O*NET and domain-specific ATLAS). Functional used for 41 FDEs where the outcome/activity matters more than vocabulary similarity — particularly for Abilities (cognitive traits) and Work Activities (behavioral descriptions). No syntactic matches expected given vocabulary divergence.
rationale_consistency_check.result: PASS — Rationale types consistently applied per use case justification.

relationship_distribution.analysis: Distribution is consistent with expectations for a general-purpose framework (O*NET) mapped against a domain-specific framework (ATLAS). High 'Intersects with' rate (65%) reflects O*NET's broader scope intersecting with ATLAS's deeper domain coverage. 'Superset of' (14%) correctly identifies where O*NET defines broader competencies that encompass specific ATLAS KSAs. 'Equal' (3%) found only for work activities with direct ATLAS equivalents (data analysis, strategy development, team building, coaching). Zero 'Subset of' is correct — O*NET elements are always equal to or broader than individual ATLAS KSAs due to O*NET's occupation-general scope.

strength_distribution.analysis: Mean strength of 5.2 reflects the vocabulary and scope gap between a general-purpose taxonomy and a domain-specific framework. Strongest matches (8-9) are in areas where O*NET work activities directly map to ATLAS core activities (data analysis, compliance evaluation, coaching, team development, strategy development). Weakest matches (3) are where O*NET physical-world concepts have only metaphorical data-world parallels.
strength_distribution.result: PASS — Strength scores are analytically defensible.

gap_issue_quality.analysis: Six thematic gap clusters identified from 8 individual FDE gap signals. All gap issues include required fields per ADR-014 Step 4. Gaps are appropriately categorized: foundational skills gaps (communication, project management, continuous learning) vs. dispositional gaps (service orientation, conflict resolution) vs. foundational knowledge gaps (ethical reasoning). Synthesis recommendations correctly defer resolution and identify cross-framework validation criteria.
gap_issue_quality.result: PASS — Gap issues are complete and appropriately scoped.

public_facing: Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

no_relationship_audit.analysis: All 23 'No relationship' findings are correctly identified as intentionally out of ATLAS scope. No false negatives detected — every No relationship element is either a physical-world competency, a domain unrelated to data/AI work, a basic cognitive trait not differentiating for professionals, or a general office/commercial skill. None represent gaps in ATLAS coverage.
no_relationship_audit.result: PASS — All No relationship findings are justified.

overall_assessment.summary: STRM-001-ONET meets all QA/QC criteria. 126 FDEs exhaustively evaluated with documented rationale. Relationship types and strength scores are analytically coherent. Six gap signals identified with complete issue context and synthesis recommendations. The O*NET STRM successfully validates ATLAS's core domain coverage while identifying targeted enrichment opportunities in foundational professional skills.
key_findings: ATLAS has strong coverage of O*NET's data-relevant competencies — 103 of 126 FDEs have documented relationships.
The 'Intersects with' dominance (65%) correctly reflects the general-vs-specific framework relationship.
Four 'Equal' relationships confirm ATLAS covers core work activities (data analysis, strategy, team development, coaching) at equivalent depth.

Gap signals cluster around foundational professional skills (communication, project management, continuous learning) rather than domain-specific technical gaps — suggesting the v0.5.2 domain pool is technically comprehensive.
No ATLAS KSA domains were found to be systematically underrepresented in the O*NET mapping.
